Title: Account Management - Informational Parent Hierarchy in D365
Description: As a System I want an informational parent grouping in D365 So that I can understand enterprise relationships without impacting Fusion.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC-1: Given multiple service accounts belong to one organization, When viewed in D365, Then hierarchy is in place on Accounts for the user to be able to add a relation between two accounts. AC-2: Given the requirement is implemented, When the user adds a parent Account Then no parent hierarchy data is sent back to Fusion. Technical Notes: The parent account field on the account record will be used to set a parent account for service and billing accounts. There is no integration or migration for this field. This is newly introduced in D365 to group all related accounts and will be manually created in D365. This does not exist today in Fusion, so the two tasks associated with this story are: A parent account record must be created - this is not a service account OR billing account just a record to connect all related accounts, the required fields will be set manually though they will not be used in any existing business logic. The parent field on the service and billing account records must be set to this manually in D365 To achieve both a & b Create a new security role called Operational Admin (note, this is also mentioned in one another work item) as a copy of one of OOB least privilege security role (basic role?) Assign the privileges - organization level, can create account, update account, read account, append and append to. As there is no integration back from D365 to Fusion AC-2 is satisfied.

Title: Account Management - Bill‑To to Ship‑To Relationship
Description: As a System I want bill‑to and ship‑to relationships visible in D365 So that billing reflects customer preferences.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC-1: Given a bill‑to is linked to multiple ship‑tos in Fusion, When synced with D365 , Then an equivalent relationship will be represented in D365 where one or more ship to (service account) is linked to a Bill to (billing account). AC-2: GIVEN a Bill to is also a Ship to in fusion When synced with D365 Then an equivalent relationship will be represented in D365. Technical notes: The current data model provides this relationship: Account (Service account) has service address field which is the SHIP to address -> This record has a child function location record, which also has a service address. Both are set to same value during the Fusion to D365 sync. The functional location record may indicate the type as room, site, floor to make it easy to group the customer assets. (A service account represents a SHIP to equivalent of Fusion) When an account is both Billing and Service account, The billing account will be a separate record - this will be created by the integration process The service account will be a separate record and its field "Billing account" will point to the billing account record. @Baumberger, Lina (Avanade) , @Bhatt, Aditi (Avanade) - Tweaked AC-2 to represent the data separation/split when Accounts are synced from Fusion to D365. The alt key on Account must be: Account Number + Site Number (SHIP to or Bill to in Fusion) + custom field to indicate Billing Account or Service Account.

Title: Knowledge Articles - Knowledge Article Versioning and Auditability
Description: As a knowledge owner, I want version history for articles so that changes are traceable and auditable.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC‑1 Given a knowledge article is updated, When a user views article details Then the last updated date and version information are visible. AC‑2 Given an article has been revised multiple times, When an administrator reviews the article, Then prior versions can be identified for audit or reference purposes. Technical Notes: D365 KB article record form will show Modified data (this is the last updated date) and major and minor versions. This along with the "published" status supports versioning and auditing. This is native functionality. On the summary tab of the LB article the grid "related versions" will show all previous versions. Outcome of this story - Document (copy from learn) with screenshot so that OPEX user can get this info in the document.

Title: Contact Management- Support Contacts Linked to Multiple Sites
Description: As a System I want one contact linked to multiple sites(functional Location D365) So that we can avoid creating duplicate contact records as it is now being done in Fusion.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC-2: Given a Fusion contact supports multiple sites in Fusion, When the contact is synced to D365, Then the contact exists once and can be related to multiple accounts and locations. Technical Notes: Based on the finalized data model, associating one contact record to multiple functional location is possible by splitting the service account and billing accounts (SHIP to and BILL to) Confirm with Avinash (OPEX) if contacts in Fusion has a unique ID, if yes the alt key would be that unique value. We can disable the OOB dup detection rule for Contact. Since this data is synced from Fusion and becomes read-only in D365, the following must be a task for OPEX team such that When data is synced over from Fusion: the BILL to site is created as Billing account in D365 and Service account is linked to Billing account. the BILLING and SERVICE accounts will have a field to clearly show if they are BILLING/SERVICE accounts. This field along with Account Number becomes the primary key In D365, Duplicate detection rule is modified to for 2b. even though this is read-only in D365.

AC:2 ---> NO WORK NEEDED Given a Service Account is located within a Region When site is added in D365, Then the region is assigned to the Service Account. Technical Notes: Add service territory, account type, billing account field in the Active Account view Add service territory, account type, billing account field in the Account Lookup and associated view

Title: Knowledge Articles - Structured Knowledge Authoring and Approval Workflow
Description: As a knowledge contributor, I want New articles or an update to the existing Article within D365 reviewed before publishing so that content quality is consistent.
AcceptanceCriteria: AC‑1 Given a new KB article is created in D365, When it is submitted for publication, Then it is reviewed by an appropriate subject matter expert before becoming visible within the search. AC‑2 Given an KB article is published, When technicians view the article, Then they can provide feedback or comments on usefulness. Note : OOTB KB BPF will not be used. Need to remove it. Technical Notes: Document knowledge article publishing process (OOB). Document how article can be assigned to another CRM user for review before publishing For the second one, document D365 feedback and comments feature so this goes for review to the Knowledge Manager before publishing. Remove OOB BPF as mentioned in the acceptance criteria.

Title: Knowledge Articles - Documentation Watermarking Strategy - SPIKE
Description: Support a strategy to apply dynamic identity‑based watermarking when service documentation is downloaded. TBD - Attributes related to Identity based Watermarking(e.g Name, IP address etc.)
AcceptanceCriteria: AC1 – Controlled Download GIVEN a user attempts to download service documentation from within the D365 KB article preview WHEN the document is downloaded THEN the downloaded articles should have the watermark on them. AC2 – Validation Required GIVEN watermarking is enabled WHEN downloaded document is accessed THEN the document should have a identity-based watermark. Technical Notes: This needs a spike in sprint 1/2 to come up with an approach and decide the scope. Based on viable approach and confirmation with OPEX, we must proceed. The watermark is the logged in CRM user's name and date time.

GIVEN a D365 user sends an email from within D365 WHEN a D365 user Looks at the email record THEN only relevant fields are available on the form.
